en:
After days of hard work, a traveler sat down on the plane and fell asleep at once.
The cabin was quiet when the aircraft left the runway.
He slept so deeply that he felt nothing during take-off.
When he woke up, the plane was already flying high above the clouds.
He looked around in confusion and asked the stewardess what had happened.
She smiled and told him he had slept through the most exciting part of the flight.
cn:
连续工作多天后，一位旅客刚在飞机上坐下就立刻睡着了。
飞机离开跑道时，机舱里很安静。
他睡得太沉，起飞过程一点感觉都没有。
等他醒来时，飞机已经在云层上方平稳飞行。
他困惑地环顾四周，问空姐到底发生了什么。
空姐笑着告诉他，他把飞行中最刺激的部分全睡过去了。

en:
In a strange city, I asked a man to show me the way to my hotel.
He kindly stopped a taxi and told the driver where to go.
The driver took me through street after street and never seemed to arrive.
At last he stopped not far from where I had started.
Then he asked for an absurd fare of three hundred pounds.
Only later did I realize I had been taken for a very expensive ride.
cn:
在一座陌生城市里，我请一名男子告诉我去酒店的路。
他热心地拦下一辆出租车，并告诉司机目的地。
司机带我一条街接一条街地绕来绕去，始终没有到达。
最后他停下时，离我出发的地方其实并不远。
接着他向我索要离谱的三百英镑车费。
直到后来我才意识到，自己被人狠狠兜了一圈。

en:
A woman decided to go on a strict diet and promised to eat less.
All day she refused cakes and told herself she was strong.
That evening her friends invited her to an elegant restaurant.
She said she was going to order only a small salad.
But the smell of roast meat and sweet desserts defeated her will.
By the end of dinner, she had eaten a full meal and planned to start again tomorrow.
cn:
一位女士决定严格节食，并承诺自己要少吃。
白天她拒绝了蛋糕，不断告诉自己要有自制力。
到了晚上，朋友邀请她去一家高档餐厅。
她说自己本来只打算点一小份沙拉。
但烤肉和甜点的香味很快击败了她的意志。
晚餐结束时，她吃了一整套大餐，并打算明天重新开始。

en:
When I moved into the new flat, my upstairs neighbors were making carpets by hand.
Every evening they carried colorful wool into their room.
At first the place looked messy, and threads were everywhere.
I often watched them quietly as they worked on beautiful patterns.
After a few weeks they had finished several carpets.
Seeing the final products, I understood that the chaos had been part of careful art.
cn:
我搬进新公寓时，楼上的邻居正在手工织地毯。
每天傍晚他们都会把彩色羊毛搬进房间。
起初那里看起来很凌乱，到处都是线团。
我常常安静地看他们一点点织出精美图案。
几周后，他们已经完成了好几张地毯。
看到成品后我才明白，那些杂乱其实是细致艺术的一部分。

en:
After a forest fire, workers returned to clear the burned area.
They found many dead snakes among the black ash.
A few days later, one man noticed another snake hanging from a branch.
He thought it was dead and picked it up carelessly.
Suddenly it moved, and everyone jumped back in fear.
The heat had stunned it earlier, but it had not died at all.
cn:
森林大火后，工人们回到现场清理被烧毁的区域。
他们在黑色灰烬中发现了许多死蛇。
几天后，一名工人又看到一条蛇挂在树枝上。
他以为那条蛇已经死了，便随手把它拿起来。
蛇突然动了一下，所有人都吓得后退。
原来它先前只是被高温烫昏了，并没有真正死亡。

en:
At a party, a valuable diamond disappeared and a detective was called.
Guests were whispering nervously while he searched each purse.
One elegant lady looked calm, but she kept rubbing her fingers.
The detective noticed something sticky on her hands.
After he questioned her, she explained that she had been serving syrup cakes.
Her sticky fingers were only kitchen evidence, not proof of theft.
cn:
在一场宴会上，一颗名贵钻石不见了，侦探被紧急叫来。
他逐个搜查手提包时，宾客们正在紧张低声交谈。
一位打扮优雅的女士看似镇定，却不停搓手指。
侦探注意到她手上有黏糊糊的痕迹。
追问后她解释，自己一直在端带糖浆的蛋糕。
她手指发黏只是厨房留下的痕迹，并不是偷窃证据。

en:
A farmer dreamed for several nights that gold was hidden in his chimney.
At midnight he finally got up and broke the bricks open.
His family watched him dig for hours with great excitement.
At last a rusty metal box fell out of the wall.
Everyone expected coins of gold inside.
When they opened it, they found only a few old tin coins and deep disappointment.
cn:
一位农夫连续几晚梦见烟囱里藏着黄金。
到了半夜，他终于起身把砖墙砸开。
家人兴奋地看着他挖了好几个小时。
最后，一个生锈的金属盒从墙里掉了出来。
所有人都以为里面会是金币。
盒子打开后，他们只看到几枚旧锡币，满心失望。

en:
A navy pilot was flying at very high speed during a night mission.
Soon he heard a strange knocking sound behind his seat.
The noise grew louder and faster as he continued the flight.
He checked every instrument, but everything looked normal.
After landing, engineers examined the aircraft carefully.
They discovered that a loose metal plate had been vibrating in the airflow.
cn:
一名海军飞行员在夜间任务中以极高速度飞行。
不久他听到座位后方传来奇怪的敲击声。
随着飞行继续，这声音变得更响也更快。
他检查了所有仪表，但数据显示都正常。
降落后，工程师对飞机进行了细致检查。
他们发现是一块松动金属板一直在气流中振动。

en:
A woman dropped her purse in a crowded street without noticing it.
A young man ran after her and politely returned it.
She thanked him warmly and put the purse into her bag.
At home she opened it and found no money inside.
Then she understood what had happened in the crowd.
The thief had stolen the cash first and then played the role of a helpful stranger.
cn:
一位女士在拥挤街头掉了钱包，却没有察觉。
一个年轻男子追上她，礼貌地把钱包还给她。
她连声道谢后，把钱包放回自己的包里。
回到家打开时，她发现里面一分钱都没有。
这时她才明白人群中究竟发生了什么。
小偷先偷走现金，再假装成热心路人把空钱包还给她。

en:
A shop owner had to close his small store because road work blocked the street.
He thought his business was going to fail completely.
For weeks he worried and planned to change his job.
Then a large shopping center was built nearby.
More people began passing his door every day.
What looked like bad luck at first became a blessing in disguise.
cn:
因为道路施工封路，一位店主不得不暂时关闭小店。
他原以为自己的生意会彻底失败。
几个星期里，他一直焦虑，甚至打算转行。
后来附近却建起了一座大型商场。
每天经过他店门口的人开始越来越多。
最初看似倒霉的事，最后反而成了因祸得福。

en:
Rex the dog scratched the door whenever he was left outside.
As soon as his owner let him in, he wanted to go out again.
This happened over and over all day long.
The barking was too loud for anyone to ignore.
No one was patient enough to enjoy a quiet afternoon.
By evening the whole family felt tired of opening the same door.
cn:
小狗 Rex 每次被留在门外就会不停抓门。
主人刚把它放进来，它又立刻想出去。
这种情况一整天反复上演。
它叫得太大声，谁也无法忽视。
大家都没有足够耐心去享受安静的下午。
到了晚上，全家都被这扇反复开关的门折腾得筋疲力尽。

en:
In a market, a fortune-teller examined my palm and spoke confidently.
He said I would soon meet a stranger who could change my life.
I walked away and did not really believe him.
That same afternoon, I met an unfamiliar traveler at the station.
Our conversation led to a surprising job opportunity.
Then I remembered the prediction and wondered whether the future had been written already.
cn:
在集市上，一位算命先生看着我的手相，自信地开口。
他说我很快会遇到一个能改变我生活的陌生人。
我离开时并不太相信他的话。
可就在当天下午，我在车站遇到了一位陌生旅客。
这次交谈竟带来了一份意外的工作机会。
那一刻我想起了预言，不禁怀疑未来是否早已写好。
